modules.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry
documentation:
As an open source project, we collect anonymous usage statistics to
prioritize and find product gaps.
This is optional and may be turned off by changing the configuration file:
 inside ~/.ploomber/stats/config.yaml
 Change stats_enabled to False.
See the user stats page for more information:
https://docs.ploomber.io/en/latest/community/user-stats.html

The data we collect is limited to:
1. The Ploomber version currently running.
2. a generated UUID, randomized when the initial install takes place,
    no personal or any identifiable information.
3. Environment variables: OS architecture, Python version etc.
4. Information about the different product phases:
    installation, API calls and errors.

    Relational schema for the telemetry.
    event_id - Unique id for the event
    action - Name of function called i.e. `execute_pipeline_started`
    (see: fn telemetry_wrapper)
    client_time - Client time
    elapsed_time - Total time from start to end of the function call
    pipeline_name_hash - Hash of pipeline name, if any
    python_version - Python version
    num_pipelines - Number of pipelines in repo, if any
    metadata - More information i.e. pipeline success (boolean)
    telemetry_version - Telemetry version

functions.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry.get_home_dir
documentation:
Checks if ploomber home was set through the env variable.
returns the actual home_dir path.

functions.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry.check_dir_exist
documentation:
Checks if a specific directory exists, creates if not.
In case the user didn't set a custom dir, will turn to the default home

functions.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry.check_telemetry_enabled
documentation:
Check if the user allows us to use telemetry. In order of precedence:

1. If the CI (GtiHub Actions) or READTHEDOCS env var is set, return False
2. If PLOOMBER_STATS_ENABLED defined, check its value
3. Otherwise use the value in stats_enabled in the config.yaml file

functions.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry.check_first_time_usage
documentation:
The function checks for first time usage if the conf file exists and the
uid file doesn't exist.

functions.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry.get_latest_version
documentation:
The function checks for the latest available ploomber version
uid file doesn't exist.

functions.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry.is_cloud_user
documentation:
The function checks if the cloud api key is set for the user.
Checks if the cloud_key is set in the User conf file (config.yaml).
returns True/False accordingly.

functions.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry.email_registered
documentation:
The function checks if the email is set for the user.
Checks if the user_email is set in the User conf file (config.yaml).
returns True/False accordingly.

functions.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry.check_version
documentation:
The function checks if the user runs the latest version
This check will be skipped if the version_check_enabled is set to False
If it's not the latest, notifies the user and saves the metadata to conf
Alerting every 2 days on stale versions

functions.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry.check_cloud
documentation: Displays a message to ask the user to sign up for Ploomber Cloud

functions.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry._get_telemetry_info
documentation:
The function checks for the local config and uid files, returns the right
values according to the config file (True/False). In addition it checks
for first time installation.

functions.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry.is_first_arg_self
documentation:
Check the func is defined inside a class

1. If the self as its first argument, it's a method
2. Otherwise, it's a function

functions.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry._map_parameters_in_fn_call_from_signature
documentation:
Based on function signature, parse args to to convert them to key-value
pairs and merge them with kwargs
Any parameter found in args that does not match the function signature
is still passed.
Missing parameters are filled with their default values
taken from sklearn-evaluation/util.py

methods.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry.UserSettings.get_cloud_key
documentation:
Get the cloud key. Returns the value in PLOOMBER_CLOUD_KEY if set,
otherwise returns the value in the config file
User-customizable settings

Parameters

version : str

print_cloud_message : bool, default=True

a package with the given name

methods.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry.Telemetry.log_api
documentation:
This function logs through an API call, assigns parameters
if missing like timestamp, event id and stats information.

methods.name: ploomber_core.telemetry.telemetry.Telemetry.log_call
documentation:
Log function call

# Parameters

action

: The action taken by the user. If None, it'll use the function's name

payload

: If True, the function will be called with `payload` as its first
  argument (a dictionary), yoyu may add values to it and they will
  be logged

log_args

: If True, function parameters a logger (but only
  bool, int, float, str, tuple, and set)

ignore_args

: A set of parameters to ignore, it only has effect when `log_args=True`

group

: An arbitrary string to group events. You may use this to group calls
  to methods in the same class

# Examples

Log function call:

```pycon
>>> from ploomber_core.telemetry import Telemetry
>>> telemetry = Telemetry("APIKEY", "packagename", "0.1")
>>> @telemetry.log_call()
... def add(x, y):
...     return x + y
>>> add(x=1, y=2)
3
```

Customize action name (by default, it'll use the name of the function):

```pycon
>>> from ploomber_core.telemetry import Telemetry
>>> telemetry = Telemetry("APIKEY", "packagename", "0.1")
>>> @telemetry.log_call(action="sum")
... def add(x, y):
...     return x + y
>>> add(x=1, y=2)
3
```

Log extra data:

```pycon
>>> from ploomber_core.telemetry import Telemetry
>>> telemetry = Telemetry("APIKEY", "packagename", "0.1")
>>> @telemetry.log_call(payload=True)
... def add(payload, x, y):
...     payload["key"] = "value to log"
...     return x + y
>>> add(x=1, y=2)
3
```

Log input arguments:

```pycon
>>> from ploomber_core.telemetry import Telemetry
>>> telemetry = Telemetry("APIKEY", "packagename", "0.1")
>>> @telemetry.log_call(log_args=True)
... def add(x, y):
...     return x + y
>>> add(x=1, y=2)
3
```

Ignore some input arguments:

```pycon
>>> from ploomber_core.telemetry import Telemetry
>>> telemetry = Telemetry("APIKEY", "packagename", "0.1")
>>> @telemetry.log_call(log_args=True, ignore_args={"y"})
... def add(x, y):
...     return x + y
>>> add(x=1, y=2)
3
```

Log method calls in a class (creating a group will add the class name
to all actions):

```pycon
>>> from ploomber_core.telemetry import Telemetry
>>> telemetry = Telemetry("APIKEY", "packagename", "0.1")
>>> telemetry_my_class = telemetry.create_group("MyClass")
>>> class MyClass:
...     @telemetry_my_class.log_call()
...     def add(self, x, y):
...         return x + y
>>> obj = MyClass()
>>> obj.add(x=1, y=2)
3
```

Unit testing (check the `_telemetry` attribute):

```pycon
>>> from ploomber_core.telemetry import Telemetry
>>> telemetry = Telemetry("APIKEY", "packagename", "0.1")
>>> @telemetry.log_call()
... def add(x, y):
...     return x + y
>>> add._telemetry["action"]
'packagename-add'
>>> add._telemetry["payload"]
False
>>> add._telemetry["log_args"]
False
>>> add._telemetry["ignore_args"]
set()
>>> add._telemetry["group"]
```
NOTE: should we log differently depending on the error type?
NOTE: how should we handle chained exceptions?

functions.name: ploomber_core.validate._is_set_or_sequence
documentation:
Checks if obj is a sequence or a set.
Returns False if obj is a string.

functions.name: ploomber_core.validate.keys
documentation:
Checks if given values are valid. If not, raises an error with
suggested valid values.

Parameters
----------
valid : list
    A list of valid values

passed : str, list, tuple, or a set
    The value to check

required : list
    Required values

name : str, default 'spec'
    The name of the parameter

show_matches : bool, default True
    Show suggested values in the error message

Raises
------
ValidationError if passed elements are not in valid

functions.name: ploomber_core.validate.get_formatted_close_matches
documentation: Returns a formatted string of the best "good enough" matches

functions.name: ploomber_core.exceptions.modify_exceptions
documentation:
A decorator that catches ValueError, TypeError, ModifiableException and modifies
the original error message

# Notes

:::{versionadded} 0.1.1
:::

:::{versionchanged} 0.2.10 Decorator also modifies exceptions with if they have a "modify_exception" attribute and it's set to True
:::

from chained exceptions

functions.name: ploomber_core.dependencies.requires
documentation:
Decorator to check if packages were imported, raise ModuleNotFoundError with an
appropriate message for missing ones

Error message:
a, b are required to use function. Install them by running pip install a b

Parameters
----------
pkgs : list
    The names of the packages required

name
    The name of the module/function/class to show in the error message,
    if None, the decorated function __name__ attribute is used

extra_msg
    Append this extra message to the end

pip_names : list
    Pip package names to show in the suggested "pip install {name}"
    command, use it if different to the package name itself

functions.name: ploomber_core.dependencies.check_installed
documentation:
A function to check if packages were imported, raise ModuleNotFoundError with an
appropriate message for missing ones

Error message:
a, b are required to use function. Install them by running pip install a b

Parameters
----------
pkgs : list
    The names of the packages required

name
    The name of the module/function/class to show in the error message,
    if None, the decorated function __name__ attribute is used

extra_msg
    Append this extra message to the end

pip_names : list
    Pip package names to show in the suggested "pip install {name}"
    command, use it if different to the package name itself

functions.name: ploomber_core.warnings.deprecation_warning
documentation:
Raise deprecation warning message, also log to posthog with telemetry instance
is provided

Parameters
----------
module_telemetry : Telemetry
    The telemetry instance defined in ploomber_core.telemetry.Telemetry

action : str
    The action to log to posthog. example: deprecated-feature-warning

message : str
    The warning message displayed to the user
